title: ranunculus paludosus var. flavescens
intro: Ranunculus paludosus var. flavescens, also known as yellow marsh buttercup, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and basal leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are attributed to its content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus paludosus var. flavescens include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
